request: Read-only audit of installer/release verification after adding verify:codex-loop, focusing install.ps1, docs/INSTALL.md, and package scripts; check new PC install runs the right Codex loop verification with portable npm/path.
decisions: Treat direct install.ps1 portable npm/path wiring as correct: it calls the portable npm.cmd and prepends the portable Node root to PATH before verify:os and verify:codex-loop.
Do not run full npm verification during this audit because the user requested read-only work and verify scripts write reports/temp data.
Report release verification gaps separately from the direct install path.
next_steps: Add a release/build assertion that the embedded install.ps1 contains verify:codex-loop or performs a dry-run/default argument check.
Decide whether install.ps1 should continue supporting no-Git ZIP fallback now that verify:codex-loop requires git, or make Git an explicit hard preflight in the script path too.
Update docs/INSTALL.md update and verification sections to include verify:codex-loop.
